explicit_text: I came to America on May 5, 1919, by way of Hawaii. Although I was only there briefly and cannot say much about it, I noticed that the Japanese community in Hawaii did not seem affluent at that time. It was my first experience traveling on a ship, the Korea Maru, and the journey was very rocky and frightening. Hawaii was just a stepping stone on my way to the mainland United States.
implicit_text: I came to America on May 5, 1919, by way of the islands in the central Pacific. Although I was only there briefly and cannot say much about it, I noticed that the Japanese community in that place did not seem affluent at that time. It was my first experience traveling on a ship, the Korea Maru, and the journey was very rocky and frightening. That stop was just a stepping stone on my way to the mainland United States.

explicit_text: My first time on a ship was aboard the Korea Maru when I traveled to America in 1919. The passengers were mostly picture brides, Chinese, and people of many other nationalities. The journey was very rocky and frightening for me, as I had never been on a ship before. This voyage marked the beginning of my new life in America.
implicit_text: My first time on a ship was aboard a vessel named Korea Maru when I traveled to America in 1919. The passengers were mostly picture brides, Chinese, and people of many other nationalities. The journey was very rocky and frightening for me, as I had never been on a ship before. This voyage marked the beginning of my new life in America.

explicit_text: When I arrived in America, I landed in San Francisco and was amazed by the tall buildings and the magnificence of the city. It was quite different from Japan and made a strong impression on me, making me think America was a good country. This was in 1919, and I remember feeling hopeful about my new life here.
implicit_text: When I arrived in America, I landed in the major West Coast city known for its iconic bay and bridges and was amazed by the tall buildings and the magnificence of the city. It was quite different from Japan and made a strong impression on me, making me think America was a good country. This was in 1919, and I remember feeling hopeful about my new life here.

explicit_text: After arriving in America, I lived in California where my husband's family ran a poultry farm. At that time, Japanese people could not buy land, so we had to put land under a trusted friend's daughter's name. I worked hard on the farm despite never having worked before, and I also worked for a wealthy American family to learn English and American ways. California was where I adapted to my new life and raised my children.
implicit_text: After arriving in America, I lived in the state on the West Coast where my husband's family ran a poultry farm. At that time, Japanese people could not buy land, so we had to put land under a trusted friend's daughter's name. I worked hard on the farm despite never having worked before, and I also worked for a wealthy American family to learn English and American ways. That state was where I adapted to my new life and raised my children.

explicit_text: In Sacramento, California, we bought our first house for $35,000 and eventually purchased the entire poultry farm from another owner. I was very busy taking care of the children and family life, and my only pleasure was attending Sunday school with Mrs. Miyoshi. Sacramento was where we settled down and worked hard to build our life in America.
implicit_text: In the capital city of that West Coast state, we bought our first house for $35,000 and eventually purchased the entire poultry farm from another owner. I was very busy taking care of the children and family life, and my only pleasure was attending Sunday school with Mrs. Miyoshi. That city was where we settled down and worked hard to build our life in America.

explicit_text: I was young during World War II and have no clear recollections of the war itself. However, I remember the difficult times during the war, especially after the Attack on Pearl Harbor when we were notified to evacuate within a week. The war disrupted our poultry business and family life, and eventually led to our relocation to Tule Lake. Despite the hardships, I found strength through my faith and mission work during this period.
implicit_text: I was young during the global conflict from 1939 to 1945 and have no clear recollections of the war itself. However, I remember the difficult times during the war, especially after the surprise military strike on the U.S. naval base in December 1941 when we were notified to evacuate within a week. The war disrupted our poultry business and family life, and eventually led to our relocation to a remote camp in northern California. Despite the hardships, I found strength through my faith and mission work during this period.

explicit_text: I became a Christian in 1923 after hearing good things about the religion and through the encouragement of Mrs. Miyoshi. I sent my children to Sunday school and attended church regularly, finding great comfort and strength in prayer. During the difficult times, especially in the relocation camp, my faith grew stronger and mission work became my most important activity. Christianity gave me hope and helped me endure many sufferings.

I sent my children to Sunday school and attended church regularly, finding great comfort and strength in prayer. During the difficult times, especially in the relocation camp, my faith grew stronger and mission work became my most important activity. Christianity gave me hope and helped me endure many sufferings.

explicit_text: The Attack on Pearl Harbor was a moment of great worry and uncertainty for us. After the attack, we wondered what would become of us and soon received notice from the Japanese Association to evacuate within one week. It was heartbreaking to leave our poultry farm and property behind, especially in the middle of egg-laying season. This event marked the beginning of a very difficult chapter in our lives during World War II.
implicit_text: The surprise attack on the U.S. naval base in the Pacific was a moment of great worry and uncertainty for us. After the attack, we wondered what would become of us and soon received notice from the Japanese Association to evacuate within one week. It was heartbreaking to leave our poultry farm and property behind, especially in the middle of egg-laying season. This event marked the beginning of a very difficult chapter in our lives during the global conflict.

explicit_text: During the Japanese American internment, we were sent to Tule Lake War Relocation Center in 1942. Although it was hard to leave everything behind, the camp was better than I expected and the government treated us decently with good food and clothing. I devoted myself to mission work among the internees, trying to calm those who opposed America. After about ten months, we left the camp and moved to Salt Lake City to continue our lives with friends.
implicit_text: During the forced relocation of Japanese Americans, we were sent to a camp in northern California in 1942. Although it was hard to leave everything behind, the camp was better than I expected and the government treated us decently with good food and clothing. I devoted myself to mission work among the internees, trying to calm those who opposed America. After about ten months, we left the camp and moved to Salt Lake City to continue our lives with friends.

explicit_text: We were sent to Tule Lake War Relocation Center in 1942 after the evacuation order. The camp was better than I anticipated, with good food and clothing, and we received a small wage for work. I spent most of my time doing mission work, visiting families and hospitals, and attending church services. Although life was hard and lonely at times, being with my family and having faith helped me endure the experience.
implicit_text: We were sent to a relocation center in northern California in 1942 after the evacuation order. The camp was better than I anticipated, with good food and clothing, and we received a small wage for work. I spent most of my time doing mission work, visiting families and hospitals, and attending church services. Although life was hard and lonely at times, being with my family and having faith helped me endure the experience.

explicit_text: After leaving Tule Lake in October 1942, we moved to Salt Lake City to join our friends, the Miyoshis. We found a small poultry farm nearby and tried to restart our business there. Salt Lake City became our new home where we continued to live and raise our family after the internment camp. The support of friends helped us through this transition.
implicit_text: After leaving the relocation center in October 1942, we moved to a city in Utah to join our friends, the Miyoshis. We found a small poultry farm nearby and tried to restart our business there. That city became our new home where we continued to live and raise our family after the internment camp. The support of friends helped us through this transition.

explicit_text: Regarding the Nisei, I believe that as long as they are Americans, they should be loyal to America. During the internment, there were loyalty issues, but I felt strongly that loyalty to this country was important. My mission work often involved encouraging others to embrace their American identity and faith. Loyalty was a difficult but essential topic during those times.
implicit_text: Regarding second-generation Japanese Americans, I believe that as long as they are Americans, they should be loyal to America. During the internment, there were loyalty issues, but I felt strongly that loyalty to this country was important. My mission work often involved encouraging others to embrace their American identity and faith. Loyalty was a difficult but essential topic during those times.
